title: Interactive Rebase — Squash and Reword
description: You've been working on a feature branch and made five small commits with mediocre messages. Before merging, the team requires a clean history: all five commits squashed into one with a properly formatted message.
objective: The feature branch `feat/user-auth` must contain exactly one commit on top of `main`. That commit's message must begin with `feat:` (conventional commit format). The working tree content must be identical to what it was with all five commits applied, and the working tree must be clean.
hints: `git rebase -i main` from the feature branch opens the interactive rebase editor.
Change `pick` to `squash` (or `s`) for commits 2–5 to fold them into commit 1.
The first commit's action should be `reword` or `r` to rename it during the rebase.
